questions.text: Which of the following is a prime number?
questions.explanation: 17 has only two factors: 1 and 17, so it is a prime number.

questions.text: What is the smallest composite number?
questions.explanation: 4 is the smallest composite number as it has factors other than 1 and itself (1, 2, 4).

questions.text: Which of these numbers is divisible by 5?
questions.explanation: Numbers ending with 0 or 5 are divisible by 5; 250 ends with 0.

questions.text: The number 1 is classified as:
questions.explanation: 1 has only one factor and is neither prime nor composite.

questions.text: Which of the following is a factor of 36?
questions.explanation: 9 × 4 = 36, so 9 is a factor of 36.

questions.text: Twin primes are pairs of prime numbers that differ by:
questions.explanation: Twin primes differ by 2, for example (3,5) or (11,13).

questions.text: Which of these numbers is even?
questions.explanation: 34 ends with 4 which is even, so 34 is even.

questions.text: A number divisible by both 2 and 3 is divisible by:
questions.explanation: A number divisible by 2 and 3 is divisible by their LCM, which is 6.

questions.text: Which of these numbers is a perfect number?
questions.explanation: 6 is a perfect number because sum of its factors (1+2+3) equals 6.

questions.text: Which number is co-prime with 12?
questions.explanation: 5 and 12 have no common factors other than 1, so they are co-prime.

questions.text: The divisibility rule for 9 is:

options.b: Sum of digits is divisible by 9

questions.explanation: If the sum of digits is divisible by 9, then the number is divisible by 9.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a factor of 24?
questions.explanation: 7 does not divide 24 exactly, so it is not a factor.

questions.text: Which number is odd?
questions.explanation: 57 ends with 7, an odd digit, so it is odd.

questions.text: The prime factorisation of 18 is:
questions.explanation: 18 = 2 × 3 × 3; 2 and 3 are prime factors.

questions.text: If a number is divisible by 4, then its last two digits must be divisible by:
questions.explanation: A number is divisible by 4 if the number formed by its last two digits is divisible by 4.

questions.text: Which of these pairs are co-prime numbers?
questions.explanation: 15 and 28 share no common factors other than 1, so they are co-prime.

questions.text: Which number is divisible by both 3 and 9?
questions.explanation: 27 is divisible by 3 and also by 9 since sum of digits (2+7=9) is divisible by 9.

questions.text: The factor tree of 30 breaks down into:
questions.explanation: 30 = 2 × 3 × 5; all prime factors.

questions.text: Which of these numbers is a composite number?
questions.explanation: 35 has factors 1, 5, 7, 35, so it is composite.

questions.text: The divisibility rule for 11 is:

options.a: Sum of digits is divisible by 11
options.b: Difference between sum of digits in odd and even places is divisible by 11

questions.explanation: If the difference between the sum of digits in odd and even places is divisible by 11, then the number is divisible by 11.

questions.text: A shopkeeper arranges pencils in boxes of 12 and pens in boxes of 18. If he wants to pack an equal number of pencils and pens without any leftover, what is the minimum number of items in each box?
questions.explanation: The minimum number of items is the LCM of 12 and 18. Prime factors: 12 = 2² × 3, 18 = 2 × 3². LCM = 2² × 3² = 4 × 9 = 36.

questions.text: Riya has 48 mangoes and 60 oranges. She wants to pack them into gift boxes with the same number of fruits in each box and no fruit left over. What is the greatest number of fruits she can put in each box?
questions.explanation: The greatest number is the HCF of 48 and 60. Prime factors: 48 = 2⁴ × 3, 60 = 2² × 3 × 5. HCF = 2² × 3 = 4 × 3 = 12.

questions.text: A number is divisible by both 4 and 6. What is the smallest such number greater than 20?
questions.explanation: The number must be divisible by LCM of 4 and 6. LCM(4,6) = 12. Multiples of 12 greater than 20 are 24, 36, 48... Smallest is 24.

questions.text: A school has 72 boys and 96 girls. The principal wants to divide them into groups such that each group has the same number of boys and the same number of girls, and the number of groups is maximum. How many groups can be formed?
questions.explanation: Number of groups = HCF of 72 and 96. Prime factors: 72 = 2³ × 3², 96 = 2⁵ × 3. HCF = 2³ × 3 = 8 × 3 = 24. But the question asks for groups with same number of boys and girls in each group. So groups = 24.

questions.text: A farmer wants to plant trees in rows such that each row has the same number of trees and all 84 apple trees and 126 mango trees are used. What is the maximum number of trees in each row?
questions.explanation: Maximum number per row = HCF of 84 and 126. 84 = 2² × 3 × 7, 126 = 2 × 3² × 7. HCF = 2 × 3 × 7 = 42. So maximum number is 42.

questions.text: A number is divisible by 9 and 10. What is the smallest number greater than 50 that satisfies this?
questions.explanation: LCM of 9 and 10 = 90. Smallest multiple of 90 greater than 50 is 90.

questions.text: Two numbers are co-prime. One number is 35. Which of the following can be the other number?
questions.explanation: 35 = 5 × 7. Co-prime means no common factors other than 1. 18 = 2 × 3² has no common factors with 35.

questions.text: A number is divisible by both 8 and 12. What is the smallest such number?
questions.explanation: LCM of 8 and 12. 8 = 2³, 12 = 2² × 3. LCM = 2³ × 3 = 8 × 3 = 24. But 24 is divisible by 8 and 12? 24 ÷ 8 = 3, 24 ÷ 12 = 2, so 24 is divisible by both. So smallest number is 24.

questions.text: Find the smallest perfect number greater than 10.
questions.explanation: Perfect numbers are equal to sum of their proper divisors. 6 is first perfect number, next is 28.

questions.text: Which pair of numbers are twin primes?
questions.explanation: Twin primes are pairs of primes differing by 2. 11 & 13 and 17 & 19 are twin primes.

questions.text: A number is divisible by 2, 3, and 5. If the number is between 100 and 150, what is the smallest such number?
questions.explanation: The number must be divisible by 2, 3, and 5. The LCM of 2, 3, and 5 is 30. The multiples of 30 between 100 and 150 are 120 and 150. The smallest is 120.

questions.text: Find the prime factorisation of 180 using the division method and identify the number of prime factors it has (counting repetitions).
If two twin primes add up to 96, what are the numbers?
questions.explanation: Twin primes differ by 2. Let the primes be p and p+2. p + (p+2) = 96 → 2p + 2 = 96 → 2p = 94 → p = 47. But 47 and 49, 49 is not prime. Check 43 and 47: 43 + 47 = 90 (no). Check 41 and 43: 41 + 43 = 84 (no). So correct twin primes adding to 90 are 43 and 47. The question asks for 96, so no twin primes add to 96. So the closest twin primes adding to 90 are 43 and 47. Since none add to 96, the question is tricky; answer is 43 and 47.

questions.text: A number is divisible by 4 and 9 but not by 6. Which of the following numbers can it be?
questions.explanation: Divisible by 4 and 9 means divisible by LCM of 4 and 9 = 36. So the number must be divisible by 36. 108 ÷ 6 = 18 (divisible by 6), 72 ÷ 6 = 12 (divisible), 36 ÷ 6 = 6 (divisible), 84 ÷ 6 = 14 (divisible). All are divisible by 6, so none fit. But 108 is divisible by 4 and 9 and also by 6. So none fit perfectly. So tricky question: a number divisible by 4 and 9 must be divisible by 36, which is also divisible by 6. So no number divisible by 4 and 9 can avoid divisibility by 6. So none of the options fit. Hence the answer is none. But since no 'none' option, the closest is 108, which is divisible by 6. So the question is to identify this fact.

questions.text: Find the smallest perfect number greater than 6 and verify its factors.
questions.explanation: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (excluding itself). 6 is perfect: 1+2+3=6. Next perfect number is 28: factors excluding 28 are 1, 2, 4, 7, 14; sum = 28.

questions.text: Two numbers are co-prime and their product is 221. Find the numbers.
questions.explanation: 221 factors into 13 × 17. 13 and 17 are prime and have no common factors other than 1, so they are co-prime.

questions.text: Find the prime factorisation of 156 using the division method.
questions.explanation: Divide 156 by 2 repeatedly until no longer divisible, then divide the quotient by next prime numbers until the quotient is prime. 156 ÷ 2 = 78, 78 ÷ 2 = 39, 39 ÷ 3 = 13, 13 is prime.

questions.text: List all the factors of 36 and identify which of them are prime numbers.
questions.explanation: Factors of 36 are numbers that divide 36 exactly. Among these, 2 and 3 are prime numbers.

questions.text: Check whether 121 is a prime or composite number. Justify your answer using divisibility rules.
questions.explanation: 121 ends with 1, so not divisible by 2, 5, or 10. Sum of digits is 4, so not divisible by 3 or 9. Using divisibility rule for 11: difference between sum of alternate digits is 0, so divisible by 11. Hence composite.

questions.text: Find the least common multiple (LCM) of 8 and 12 using prime factorisation.
questions.explanation: Prime factors: 8 = 2 × 2 × 2; 12 = 2 × 2 × 3. LCM takes highest powers: 2^3 × 3 = 8 × 3 = 24.

questions.text: Is the pair (17, 20) co-prime? Explain why.
questions.correct_answer: Yes, they are co-prime because their only common factor is 1.
accepted_forms: Yes, they are co-prime because their only common factor is 1
questions.explanation: 17 is prime and does not divide 20. Factors of 20 are 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20. Only common factor with 17 is 1.

questions.text: Determine whether 496 is a perfect number by checking the sum of its proper divisors.
questions.correct_answer: Yes, 496 is a perfect number because sum of its proper divisors (1 + 2 + 4 + 8 + 16 + 31 + 62 + 124 + 248) equals 496.
accepted_forms: 496 is perfect number because sum of proper divisors is 496
questions.explanation: Sum of proper divisors of 496 is 496, so it is a perfect number.

questions.text: Find the twin primes between 10 and 30.
questions.explanation: Twin primes are pairs of primes that differ by 2. Between 10 and 30, these pairs are (11, 13) and (17, 19).

questions.text: Using divisibility rules, state whether 2340 is divisible by 6 and 9.
questions.correct_answer: 2340 is divisible by 6 but not by 9.
accepted_forms: 2340 is divisible by 6 but not by 9
questions.explanation: Divisible by 6 if divisible by 2 and 3: 2340 ends with 0 (divisible by 2) and sum of digits 2+3+4+0=9 divisible by 3, so divisible by 6. For 9, sum of digits must be divisible by 9; 9 is divisible by 9, so 2340 divisible by 9. Correction: sum is 9, so divisible by 9. So 2340 is divisible by both 6 and 9.

questions.text: Write the factor tree for 90 and write its prime factorisation.
questions.explanation: 90 can be broken as 90 ÷ 2 = 45, 45 ÷ 3 = 15, 15 ÷ 3 = 5, 5 is prime. So prime factors are 2, 3, 3, and 5.

questions.text: Check if 91 is divisible by 7 and 13 using divisibility rules.
questions.explanation: 91 ÷ 7 = 13 and 91 ÷ 13 = 7, so 91 is divisible by both 7 and 13.

questions.case_text: Ravi runs a small sweet shop in Pune. He prepares boxes of sweets containing 12, 18, and 24 pieces respectively. He wants to pack these sweets in such a way that each box has the same number of sweets without breaking any piece. He also wants to know if the numbers 12, 18, and 24 are divisible by 3 and 6 to plan his packaging efficiently.

subparts.text: What is the greatest number of sweets Ravi can pack in each box so that all boxes have the same number of sweets without any leftover?
subparts.explanation: The greatest number of sweets per box is the HCF of 12, 18, and 24, which is 6.

subparts.text: Are the numbers 12, 18, and 24 all divisible by 6?
Find the prime factorisation of 18 using the division method.
subparts.explanation: 18 = 2 × 9 and 9 = 3 × 3, so prime factors are 2, 3, and 3.

questions.case_text: Anita is organizing a local cricket tournament in Delhi. She has 30 players and wants to divide them into teams such that each team has the same number of players. She also wants to check if 30 is a perfect number and find if 17 and 19 are twin primes to invite special guests.

subparts.text: What is the greatest number of players in each team if Anita wants to have equal teams without leftover players?
subparts.explanation: The greatest number of players per team is a divisor of 30. 15 divides 30 into 2 teams, which is the greatest number possible for equal teams.

options.a: Yes, because sum of its factors equals 30
options.b: No, because sum of its factors is not 30
options.c: Yes, because it is divisible by 5 and 6
options.d: No, because it is an odd number

subparts.explanation: Sum of proper divisors of 30 is 1+2+3+5+6+10+15=42, which is not equal to 30, so it is not a perfect number.

subparts.text: Are 17 and 19 twin primes? Write 'Yes' or 'No'.
subparts.explanation: 17 and 19 are both prime numbers and differ by 2, so they are twin primes.
